JAISP

A joint Rubin–Euclid foundation model for cross-instrument source detection and astrometry

— and a path to Roman —

Rubin · ugrizy Euclid · VIS YJH Roman · next
Shoubaneh Hemmati · IPAC, Caltech with G. Helou, Y.-H. Lin, R. Akeson, C. D. Dowell, A. Faisst, T. Greene, D. Shupe · Hemmati et al. 2026 · github.com/xoubish/JAISP
Roman Science Conference · Caltech/IPAC · July 2026
Motivation

Why combine surveys at the pixel level?

  • Cosmology is now limited by systematics, not statistics — residual tensions between probes can neither be dismissed as noise nor attributed to new physics.
  • Rubin/LSST, Euclid, and Roman observe overlapping sky from ~0.3 to 2 μm at sub-arcsecond resolution — in combination they can calibrate and cross-check one another, suppressing survey-specific systematics no single survey controls alone.
  • But joint pixel-level processing means confronting heterogeneous PSFs, samplings, depths, and noise simultaneously — a regime where classical pipelines strain.
  • The goal is not to discard classical pipelines, but to move the joint, cross-instrument part of the problem into a representation learned from the pixels themselves.
Approach

One representation, many cheap heads

  • Train once, self-supervised: a masked-band autoencoder — hide one of the ten bands, predict it from the other nine. No labels, no catalogs, no simulations — raw public mosaics only.
  • Freeze, then reuse: every measurement is a small head (≲ few M parameters) reading cached frozen features.
  • Detection & astrometry in hand; photometry & shape heads in preparation — all from the same features.

Cost structure

2.5 GPU-hourstotal pretraining cost
Model size~9M parameters
Featuresextracted once per dataset
Each new taska small head on cached features
Each new fieldzero retraining (→ slide 12)
Data

The earliest public release of each survey

ECDFS training field · held-out EDF-S · the ten-band stack of one matched tile
  • Rubin DP1 (ComCam commissioning, ugrizy at 0.2″/px, PSF ~1″) + Euclid Q1 MER mosaics (VIS + NISP YJH at 0.1″/px) — 790 matched ten-band tiles over ~0.2 deg² of ECDFS. EDF-S is held out entirely for the transfer test.
Architecture

Each instrument at its native resolution

per-band stems → two encoder branches → fused 0.4″ bottleneck → frozen features → task heads
  • Why reconstruction, not alignment? Contrastive/JEPA objectives reward similar features but never require where — pixel reconstruction forces sub-pixel spatial layout to survive into the representation.
Validation · 1 of 2

Did it learn cross-instrument structure?

truth · reconstruction · residual — held-out tiles
  • Withhold one band on held-out tiles; predict it from the other nine.
  • Rubin r, Euclid VIS, and NISP H all reconstructed faithfully — residuals confined to bright cores and faint galaxy wings.
  • A band from one instrument, predicted from bands of the other: genuine cross-instrument correspondence, not within-instrument copying.
Validation · 2 of 2

…and is it organized physically?

decodability of the frozen features · information routing between bands
  • Every band's brightness is linearly decodable (R² ≈ 0.7–0.95) — accessible to any head. Routing mirrors real SEDs: VIS is the universal spatial scaffold; Rubin bands draw on spectral neighbors; NISP YJH form a near-closed block.
Task 1 · Detection

A detection head on frozen features

held-out VIS tile · four source lists overlaid
  • A CenterNet-style head reading the frozen fused ten-band bottleneck — learned peak-finding on a learned source-evidence image.
  • Trained on lightweight classical labels (SEP 3σ on VIS) — no curated catalog supervision needed.
  • Matches the Euclid Q1 MER catalog on held-out sky, and returns one detection per galaxy where classical extraction fragments extended systems.
Task 1 · Detection

Ten bands buy real depth

injection completeness · MER recovery · working point
+0.45 magdeeper from ten-band fusion · 50% depth VIS ≈ 25.0
d₅₀ (VIS mag)fusedstems
all 10 bands25.025.1
VIS only24.625.1
multi-band gain+0.45+0.03
  • vs. MER catalog: 93% completeness, 95% purity (a conservative floor).
  • The gain lives in the fused representation, not the head design.
Task 2 · Astrometry

Carrying VIS precision to every band

raw (dashed) vs head-corrected (solid) · offset clouds and S/N dependence
  • A 0.7M-parameter position head collapses raw ~50 mas scatter to 14–17 mas and re-centers every band. Against injected truth: 19 mas at S/N = 5 vs ~50 mas classical — within ~2 mas of the VIS anchor floor.
Task 2 · Astrometry

The concordance field: how well do two Gaia-tied surveys agree?

PINN · HGP · after head — and the field's decomposition
  • Beneath the scatter: a coherent 9–10 mas pattern between the two independently Gaia-anchored solutions — ~7 mas shared by all ten bands.
  • Recovered by two solvers of different character; the head absorbs it source-by-source (→ 1.5 mas).
  • A few-mas, degree-scale map of cross-survey agreement — QA that no single pipeline can produce.
Transfer

Zero-shot on a second deep field — nothing retrained

EDF-S, 72 tiles never seen by foundation or heads
  • Detection 93.4% / 93.1% completeness/purity vs 93.3% / 94.5% in-distribution; astrometry on the same 12–18 mas floor. Scaling to more sky is a matter of applying the stack, not rebuilding it.
Roman

Roman: the natural third stream

Built for this

  • One encoder branch per instrument, fused on a common physical grid — adding Roman = adding a third branch, not redesigning the model.
  • Pretraining needs only overlapping public pixels — no labels, no simulations; hours of GPU time once WFI imaging overlaps Rubin/Euclid sky.

Roman → the stack

  • Sharp, deep NIR at 0.11″/px — the near-infrared analog of the VIS spatial anchor, where today we lean on resampled NISP.
  • Wide-area survey over thousands of deg² inside the LSST footprint, plus deep time-domain fields.

The stack → Roman

  • Multi-band detection beyond any single-band catalog (here: +0.45 mag); WFI-anchored positions in Rubin's seeing-limited bands.
  • Three-way concordance fields — Rubin–Euclid–Roman agreement mapped at the few-mas level; native-frame registration solved inside the model (the ~3 mas NISP resampling residual is exactly this kind of inherited error).
The launch window opens August 30 — the recipe is ready.
Takeaways

One representation — tasks, fields, instruments

  • A ~9M-parameter foundation model, pretrained label-free on public Rubin + Euclid mosaics in ~2.5 GPU-hours, learns a shared ten-band representation precise enough for measurement.
  • Detection: matches the Euclid catalog (93% / 95%), and ten-band fusion pushes 0.45 mag deeper than VIS alone.
  • Astrometry: VIS-quality positions in every band — ~50 → 14–17 mas, 19 mas at S/N = 5 against injected truth — plus a few-mas concordance field between two Gaia-tied solutions.
  • The frozen stack transfers zero-shot to a second deep field — all on the earliest data both surveys have released: a floor, not a ceiling.
  • Roman slots in as a third encoder stream: a sharp NIR anchor, and three-way cross-survey calibration.
Hemmati et al. 2026 · github.com/xoubish/JAISP · shemmati@caltech.edu
